incorrects.description: Student placed correct factors but wrong product on first attempt

steps.text: You have the factors right. Now skip-count by 10s, four times, to find the product.

incorrects.description: Student placed 14 as product, added factors instead of multiplying

steps.text: You added 4 plus 10. Multiplication means groups. 4 groups of 10. Skip-count by 10s four times.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at what 4 groups of 10 means.

incorrects.description: Student placed correct factors but wrong product on second attempt

params.description: Product slot pulses. Tiles 40 highlights in palette.

steps.text: The factors are correct: 4 and 10. Now find the product. Skip-count by 10s: 10, 20, 30, 40. Which tile shows that number?

incorrects.description: Student placed 14 as product on second attempt, still adding instead of multiplying

params.description: Tile 40 pulses in palette. Product slot highlighted.

steps.text: That's adding. Multiplication is different. 4 groups of 10 means skip-count by 10s four times: 10, 20, 30, 40. What's the product?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly on second attempt

steps.text: Here's what helps: 4 groups of 10. The first number tells you how many groups. The second tells you what's in each group. Skip-count by 10s four times and find that number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Tiles 4, 10, and 40 place themselves sequentially into correct slots.

steps.text: Let me show you. 4 groups of 10 means we multiply 4 times 10. I skip-count by 10s four times: 10, 20, 30, 40. The product is 40. The complete equation is 4 times 10 equals 40.
